title: Apply Distributive Property (Division)
description: Break apart a dividend into friendly numbers and divide each part before adding the quotients.
condition: Division expression where the dividend can be decomposed (e.g., 75 ÷ 5, 36 ÷ 2).
response: Rewrite as sum of quotients and add (e.g., (50 ÷ 5) + (25 ÷ 5)).
label: Principle / Model

title: Compute Division for Equal Groups
description: Divide a total quantity by the number in each group or the number of groups to find the unknown quantity in an equal-groups situation.
condition: Given a total amount and either the number of groups or the size of each group in an equal-groups context.
response: Compute the quotient using division to find the unknown quantity.
label: Principle / Model
example: If there are 35 flowers with 7 in each row, compute $35 \div 7 = 5$ rows.
notes: Applies to both “how many groups?” and “how many in each group?” division situations.

title: Decompose a Factor Using Addition
description: Rewrite a multiplication expression by decomposing one factor into a sum.
condition: Expression of the form a × b where b can be decomposed as b1 + b2.
response: Rewrite as a × (b1 + b2).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Precursor step to applying distributive property.

title: Decompose Two-Digit Number into Tens and Ones
description: Rewrite a two-digit number as the sum of its tens and ones to support computation.
condition: Given a two-digit number (e.g., 15, 16, 96) in a computation context.
response: Express the number as tens + ones (e.g., 15 = 10 + 5, 96 = 90 + 6).
label: Principle
notes: Supports distributive strategies for multiplication and division.

title: Identify 'How Many Groups?' Division
description: Recognize situations where items are placed into equal groups of known size to find the number of groups.
condition: Word problem describes total items and group size, asking for number of groups.
response: Represent with division: total ÷ size of each group.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Identify 'How Many in Each Group?' Division
description: Recognize situations where a total is divided into equal groups to find the size of each group.
condition: Word problem describes a total split into a known number of equal groups, asking for amount in each group.
response: Represent with division: total ÷ number of groups.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Identify Equal Groups Multiplication
description: Recognize situations describing equal groups and represent with multiplication.
condition: Word problem gives number of groups and number in each group, asking for total.
response: Represent with multiplication: groups × number in each group.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Interpret Array as Multiplication
description: Recognize that rows and columns in an array represent factors in a multiplication expression.
condition: A rectangular array with equal rows and equal number of items per row.
response: State total as (number of rows) × (number per row).
label: Principle / Model

title: Interpret Division as an Unknown Factor
description: Understand division as finding a missing factor in a multiplication equation.
condition: Situation with total a and number of equal groups b, asking for size of each group.
response: Represent as b × ? = a or a ÷ b = ?.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 24 students are in 3 equal rows, write $3 \times ? = 24$ or $24 \div 3 = ?$.

title: Interpret Division as Equal Groups
description: Understand division as sharing a total equally into a given number of groups.
condition: A context describing a total amount split into equal groups (e.g., blocks in bins).
response: Set up and compute total ÷ number of groups to find amount per group.
label: Principle
example: If 92 blocks are placed into 4 equal bins, compute $92 \div 4 = 23$ blocks per bin.
notes: Conceptual meaning of division in word problems.

title: Match Equation to Situation
description: Determine whether an equation correctly represents a described situation.
condition: Given a word problem and multiple candidate equations using same numbers.
response: Select equations whose structure matches the situation context.
label: Principle / Model

title: Multiply One-Digit Number by Multiple of Ten
description: Multiply a one-digit number by a multiple of 10 using place-value reasoning.
condition: Expression of the form a × (b × 10), where a and b are one-digit numbers.
response: Compute (a × b) × 10 (i.e., multiply basic fact and append a zero).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Relies on place-value structure of tens.

title: Recall Basic Division Fact
description: Recall or compute a division fact within 100.
condition: Division expression with whole numbers within 100 (e.g., 80 ÷ 8, 36 ÷ 2).
response: State the correct quotient.
label: Production / Skill

title: Recall/Compute Basic Multiplication Facts
description: Find the product of two one-digit numbers.
condition: Expression of the form a × b, where a and b are one-digit numbers.
response: State the product a × b.
label: Production / Skill

title: Relate Multiplication and Division Facts
description: Use a known multiplication fact to determine a related division fact or missing factor.
condition: Known product a × b = c.
response: State related facts c ÷ a = b or c ÷ b = a.
label: Principle / Model

title: Represent Equal Groups with a Drawing
description: Create a visual model (such as rows or equal groups) that shows the total partitioned into equal groups consistent with the problem context.
condition: Given a word problem describing a total amount arranged into equal groups.
response: Draw equal groups (e.g., rows or shelves) showing the correct number in each group and/or total number of groups.
label: Production / Skill
example: Draw 5 rows with 7 dots in each row to represent $35$ flowers arranged $7$ per row.
notes: Accuracy depends on matching the structure of the situation (groups vs. group size).

title: Represent Unknown with Variable
description: Use a letter to represent an unknown quantity in an equation.
condition: Word problem asking for an unknown quantity.
response: Define a variable (e.g., p) to represent the unknown.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Let $p$ be the number of pages left to read.

title: Sequence Operations in Two-Step Word Problem
description: Identify and execute the correct order of operations in a two-step arithmetic context.
condition: A word problem requiring two different operations to reach the solution.
response: Determine the first required operation from the context, compute it, then apply the second operation to the result.
label: Rule
notes: Focuses on procedural sequencing rather than specific operations.

title: Solve for Missing Factor
description: Find an unknown factor in a multiplication or division equation.
condition: Equation of form a × ? = b or b ÷ a = ? with known values.
response: Determine the unknown number that makes the equation true.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract Product from Total
description: After computing a product representing a part, subtract it from the whole to find the remainder.
condition: Given total T and computed part P.
response: Compute T − P.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract to Find Remaining Amount Before Division
description: In a two-step context, subtract a known part from the total before dividing the remainder.
condition: A word problem where part of a total is used or removed before equal sharing.
response: Compute total − part to find remainder, then use that value in a division step.
label: Principle
example: From 117 blocks, 25 are used: $117 - 25 = 92$. Then divide $92 \div 4 = 23$.

title: Use Known Product to Find Related Product
description: Use a known multiplication fact to compute a related product by adding or multiplying.
condition: Expression such as a × (b + 1) or a × (2b) with known value of a × b.
response: Compute new product using the known product (e.g., add another group or multiply result).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Builds derived facts from known facts.

title: Write Division Expression from Context
description: Translate an equal-groups word problem into a symbolic division expression that matches the quantities in the context.
condition: A word problem describes a total and equal grouping and asks for an equation.
response: Write a division expression using the total as the dividend and the known group quantity as the divisor.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Noah has 36 books and 4 shelves → write $36 \div 4$.
notes: Conventional symbolic representation; relies on understanding of division notation.
